documentation.value: Defines an object type with named fields. Each field has a type and optional arguments.
The name of the object type.
Defines a named query operation with optional variables for parameterized data fetching.
Optional name for the query operation.
Variable declarations with their GraphQL types.
Defines a mutation operation for creating, updating, or deleting data.
Optional name for the mutation operation.
Variable declarations with their GraphQL types.
Defines a subscription operation for real-time data updates via a persistent connection.
Optional name for the subscription operation.
Variable declarations with their GraphQL types.
Defines an input object type used for complex arguments in queries and mutations.
The name of the input type.
Defines an enumeration type restricted to a particular set of allowed values.
The name of the enum type.
Defines an abstract interface type that other types can implement, ensuring they include the specified fields.
The name of the interface.
Defines a union type representing one of several possible object types.
The name of the union type.
Defines a custom scalar type for values that aren't covered by the default scalars (Int, Float, String, Boolean, ID).
The name of the custom scalar type.
Defines a custom directive that annotates parts of a GraphQL schema or document.
The name of the directive (prefixed with @).
Optional arguments accepted by the directive.
Schema locations where the directive can be applied (e.g., FIELD, OBJECT, ARGUMENT_DEFINITION).
